title: Newtype Pattern
description: Wrap an existing type in a single-field tuple struct to create a distinct type. This gives you type safety (the compiler prevents mixing up values of the same underlying type), allows implementing traits on foreign types (orphan rule workaround), and provides a place to add validation and methods.
whenToUse: When you want type safety between values with the same underlying type (e.g., UserId vs OrderId, both u64)
When you need to implement a foreign trait on a foreign type
When you want to restrict the API of an inner type
When you want to add validation on construction
realWorldUse: std::num::NonZeroU64, std::path::PathBuf (wraps OsString), String (wraps Vec<u8> with UTF-8 guarantee). Many libraries use newtypes for IDs, validated strings, and domain-specific values.
